title: festuca marginata subsp. gallica
intro: Festuca marginata subsp. gallica, also known as Gallic fescue or Festuca gallica, is a subspecies of grass in the family Poaceae, commonly found in the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Festuca marginata subsp. gallica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and safety.
